cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The problem says that John is correct about today's date being June 19, 2019 (6/19/2019). We need to find out what the date was one year ago from today. So, the task is to subtract one year from June 19, 2019. Let me think. If today is June 19, 2019, then one year ago would be June 19, 2018, right? Because subtracting a year from the year part of the date keeps the month and day the same, assuming that the date isn't affected by the leap year or something. Wait, but does that apply here? Wait, if you subtract one year from June 19, 2019, you just subtract 1 from the year. So 2019 minus 1 is 2018. So the date would be 06/19/2018. Let me check if there's any catch here. For example, if today was January 1st, then one year ago would be December 31st of the previous year, but in this case, June 19th is not an edge case. So, June 19th, 2018 should be correct. Looking at the options, option A is 06/19/2018. But wait, the options given are: (A) 06/19/2018 (B) 05/22/2018 (C) 06/19/1934 (D) 06/19/1956 (E) 06/24/2018 (F) 06/18/2018 So option A is indeed 06/19/2018. That would be the answer. But let me double-check. Another way to think: if today is June 19, 2019, then one year ago is exactly the same day but in the previous year. Unless there's some leap year consideration. For example, if today was February 29, 2020 (a leap year), then one year ago would be February 28, 2019. But June 19th is not a leap day. So no such issue here. Therefore, one year ago from June 19, 2019 is indeed June 19, 2018. So the correct answer should be option A: 06/19/2018. But let me check the options again. Yes, option A is there.
